article.title: CMS announces 2026 wave of Medicare provider revalidation due dates
article.summary: CMS has begun mailing Medicare provider revalidation notices to practices with cycles ending Q3 2026. Providers have 60 days from the notice mail date to submit a complete CMS-855 application via PECOS. Failure to revalidate results in deactivation of billing privileges.
article.rawContent: Medicare requires every provider and supplier to revalidate their enrollment information every 5 years (every 3 years for DMEPOS suppliers) under 42 CFR 424.515. CMS contractors are now mailing letters to providers whose revalidation due date falls in Q3 2026. Notable details: (1) the 60-day clock starts from the mail date on the letter, not receipt; (2) updates can be filed online via PECOS or on paper Form CMS-855; (3) the application must be complete and accurate — partial submissions trigger development requests that consume the 60-day window; (4) deactivated providers cannot retroactively bill for services rendered after deactivation, even if revalidation is later approved. Practices should verify their PECOS contact information and confirm notice receipt.
